title: Overseas processing of family members of in-Canada applicants for permanent residence - Canada.ca
content:
## Overseas processing of family members of in-Canada applicants for permanent residence
This section contains policy, procedures and guidance used by IRCC staff. It is posted on the department’s website as a courtesy to stakeholders.
The following program delivery instructions explain how to process family members outside Canada for the following in-Canada applicants for permanent residence:
- Spouse or common-law partner in Canada class
- Live-in caregiver class
- Family members of protected temporary residents
- Applications for permanent residence made in Canada on humanitarian or compassionate grounds
- Temporary resident permit holder class
- General requirements for overseas dependents
- Roles and responsibilities of offices processing in Canada applications for permanent residence
- Assessment of applications for overseas dependants
- Finalizing the case and issuing a visa
- Family class: Spouse or common-law partner
- Permanent residence applications from live-in caregivers
- Protected persons: Processing applications for permanent residence
- Humanitarian and compassionate consideration
